scope_note: QA Pell Rotor Eigenspread cert [297]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.IV (irrationality of sqrt(2), Pell equation), Ch.XIII (Pell solutions via continued fractions); Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions, Wild Egg Books, ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (rational spread s=e^2/(b^2+e^2), blue quadrance G_tilde). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): Let A=[[1,2],[1,1]] (det=-1, Pell step matrix). (C1) A maps (b,e)->(b+2e,b+e) exactly; det(A)=-1 (odd-grade versor). (C2) Pell chain {(b_n,e_n)} starting (b_0,e_0)=(1,1) under repeated A satisfies b_n^2-2e_n^2=(-1)^(n+1) for all n>=0 (|I|=1 always; sign alternates, starting negative: (1,1)->I=-1, (3,2)->I=+1, (7,5)->I=-1, ...). (C3) Spread s_n=e_n^2/(b_n^2+e_n^2) satisfies |s_n-1/3|=1/(3*G_tilde_n) where G_tilde_n=b_n^2+e_n^2; equivalently I_n=3*G_tilde_n*|s_n-1/3|=1 for all n, reproducing cert [292] formula with I=1. Spreads alternate above/below 1/3. (C4) No positive integer solution to b^2=2e^2 exists (sqrt(2) is irrational); verified exhaustively for b,e<=10^4. (C5) A^2=[[3,4],[2,3]] (the SL(2,Z) rotor from cert [296] SB-word RLLR); the double-step matrix has its Mobius fixed point at sqrt(2) (solving (3z+4)/(2z+3)=z gives z^2=2). Mechanism: Theorem NT maintained throughout (all arithmetic on integer pairs; spread is an observer projection from QA state). Builds on cert [292] (Koenig spread optimality, I=3G_tilde|s-1/3|) and cert [296] (SL(2,Z) versor isomorphism, RLLR matrix). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
